findings: Repetitive phrasing throughout the copy (e.g., “Roof repairs, restoration and inspections” appears multiple times).
The copy focuses heavily on *process* (inspection first) and less on the *benefits* to the homeowner.
Lack of specific examples or quantifiable results.
The 'FAQ' section is minimal and doesn't address many common roofing concerns.
The copy reads as informational rather than persuasive.
The call to action is repetitive, and the placement can be improved.

rewriteBrief.title: Reframe the title to focus on benefits, not just location. e.g., 'Protect Your Brisbane & Gold Coast Home with RidgeLine Roofing'
rewriteBrief.h1: Highlight the key benefit in the H1. E.g., 'Reliable Roofing Solutions for Brisbane & Gold Coast Homes'
rewriteBrief.body: Rewrite sections to prioritize homeowner benefits over process descriptions. For example, instead of “Inspection-first advice…”, say “We’ll thoroughly inspect your roof to identify the *best* and *most cost-effective* solution for your needs.” Add social proof (testimonials, before/after photos if possible).
rewriteBrief.buttons: Vary the button copy beyond just repeating the company name or phone number. Use action-oriented language. E.g., 'Get a Free Inspection', 'See Our Roofing Options'.
rewriteBrief.faq: Expand the FAQ section to address common concerns (cost, timeline, warranty, etc.).
rewriteBrief.localSeo: Optimize for long-tail keywords related to specific roofing problems (e.g., “leaking roof repair Brisbane”).

response.openDesignCopyBrief: Consider a layout that visually breaks up the text and emphasizes key benefits. Use images of completed projects to build trust. Prioritize mobile responsiveness.

evidence.reason: Keep QA artifact readable; JSON parse success is captured by ok=true and response object.
